meta.name: Ligand-receptor interactions
meta.description: Permutation test (CellPhoneDB-style) for ligand-receptor pairs that are co-expressed across region pairs, grouped by obs['cluster']; results land in uns['cluster_ligrec'] and are shown as a dotplot.
meta.provenance: squidpy 'Receptor-ligand analysis' example (squidpy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/notebooks/examples/graph/compute_ligrec.html). Targets the visium_hne example with an explicit set of known mouse-brain ligand-receptor pairs.

readme: Runs squidpy's re-implementation of the CellPhoneDB permutation test on an explicit list of ligand-receptor pairs, grouped by `obs['cluster']`, then plots the significant pairs. Interactions are passed in (not fetched from omnipath) so the step runs offline; the default pairs are specific to the mouse-brain panel — edit them for other datasets. `use_raw` is false because the app's extracted table has no `.raw`.

params.name: cluster_key
params.tooltip: obs column holding the region/cluster labels

params.name: interactions
params.tooltip: ligand-receptor pairs to test, as a JSON list of [ligand, receptor] pairs

params.name: n_perms
params.tooltip: permutations for the significance test (higher = slower, more precise p-values)
